content.full_text:
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 19, 1988 
Kathleen Modugno, Treasurer 
Friends of Coastal Open Space/Yes on 
Proposition C 
P. o. Box 91840 
Santa Barbara, CA 
93190 
Dear Ms. Modugno: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-223 
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1i 
QUESTIONS 
1. 
Were contributions received by Friends of Coastal Open 
Space/Yes on Proposition C ("the Committee") correctly reported as 
having been made by Concerned citizens to Save the Wilcox 
• ("Concerned Citizens")? 
2. 
Is the Committee a sponsored committee? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. 
Based on the information you have provided, it appears 
that Concerned citizens was correctly disclosed as the source of 
the contributions. 
However, if you have information which 
indicates otherwise, you should take reasonable steps to inquire 
into the true source of the contributions. 
2. 
The Committee may be sponsored by Concerned citizens. 
However, more information is needed before a determination can be 
made. 
lIGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000 et sea. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of ~ 
California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Kathleen Modugno 
Page Two 
FACTS 
Concerned citizens to Save the Wilcox, an unincorporated 
association, was awarded $30,000 in settlement of a lawsuit with 
Santa Barbara Senior Housing Associates, Inc. 
The settlement 
specified that the $30,000 was to be us'ed on a campaign to support 
Proposition C in the City of Santa Barbara. 
Through the period 
ending May 21, 1988, Concerned citizens had contributed $7,930 to 
the Friends of Coastal Open Space/Yes on Proposition C Committee. 
At that time, the $7,930 represented 40 percent of the Committee's 
total receipts. 
Additional,contributions could increase Concerned 
Citizen's percentage to 70 percent of total receipts. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act requires committees formed to support or oppose ballot 
measures to file the campaign disclosure reports set forth in 
section 84200 et seq. 
On those reports, such committees must 
provide detailed information regarding persons from whom they have 
received contributions totaling $100 or more during the calendar 
year. 
(Section 84211.) 
There is nothing in the facts you presented to indicate that 
someone other than Concerned citizens should be listed as the 
• source of the contributions received by the Committee. 
However, 
committee treasurers have an obligation to use all reasonable 
diligence in determining the true source of contributions, and are 
required by Commission regulations to: 
Correct any inaccuracies or omissions in campaign statements 
of which the treasurer knows, and cause to be checked, and if, 
necessary, corrected, any information in campaign statements 
which a person of reasonable prudence would question based on 
all the surrounding circumstances of which the treasurer is 
aware or should be aware by reason of his or her duties under 
this regulation and the Act. 
Regulation 18427. 
If you have reason to believe that Concerned citizens is 
making contributions to the Committee under circumstances other 
than the ones you described, you should seek additional 
information from them. 
In addition, please note that Section 
84211 requires the Committee to disclose, among other things, the 
name and address of each contributor of $100 or more. 
If the 
contribution was received through an intermediary, section 84302 
requires disclosure of the name and address of the intermediary as 
well. 


• 
Kathleen Modugno 
Page Three 
With regard to your second question, section 82048.5 states 
that an entity or organization sponsors a committee if any of the 
following apply: 
1. 
The committee receives all or nearly all of its 
contributions from the entity or organization or its members, 
officers, employees or shareholders; or 
2. 
The entity or organization collects contributions 
for the committee by use of payroll deductions or dues from 
its members, officers 6~ employees; or 
3. 
The entity or organization, alone or in combination 
with other organizations, provides all or nearly all of the 
administrative services for the committee; or 
4. 
The entity or organization, alone or in combination 
with other organizations, sets the policies for soliciting 
contributions or making expenditures of committee funds. 
Your letter provides insufficient facts to determine if 
Concerned citizens sponsors the Committee. 
Please feel free to 
call me at (916) 322-5662 if you would like to discuss this 
question, or if you need additional information . 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By 
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 


•

Will you pl •••• r.spond to this concern? 
Thank you For your help in these m.tters.
